title: Beej's Guide to Network Programming
author: Brian “Beej Jorgensen” Hall
description: A practical, beginner‑friendly guide to socket programming in C, covering TCP, UDP, IPv4/IPv6, and low‑level networking concepts.

title: Hands-On System Programming with Linux
author: Kaiwan N Billimoria
description: A deep dive into Linux system programming, covering processes, memory, filesystems, signals, concurrency, and low‑level OS interfaces.

title: Jumping into C++
author: Alex Allain
description: An accessible introduction to C++ that builds strong fundamentals in syntax, memory, pointers, classes, and problem‑solving.

title: Operating Systems: Three Easy Pieces
author: Remzi H. Arpaci‑Dusseau, Andrea C. Arpaci‑Dusseau
description: A foundational OS textbook explaining processes, threads, scheduling, memory virtualization, concurrency, and file systems.

title: A Primer in SQL (Third Edition)
author: R. J. Groff, P. N. Weinberg
description: A clear, structured introduction to SQL fundamentals, covering queries, joins, data modeling, and practical relational database concepts.
